evals.name: pr-22214-large-frontend-refactor
evals.prompt: Review the changes in PR #22214 (branch origin/pr/22214 targeting main). This is a large frontend refactor migrating create entity actions to use entityCreateOptionAction extensions, with deprecations.
evals.expected_output: A structured review that identifies frontend deprecation patterns, flags the large PR complexity, handles 75+ files correctly, checks for breaking changes in exported components, and produces the correct output format.

assertions.text: Review identifies deprecation patterns (@deprecated, UmbDeprecation)
Checks frontend-specific breaking changes (exports, custom elements) not just backend
Findings reference specific files with line numbers
Properly deprecated code is NOT flagged as Critical breaking change

Review does not flag purely cosmetic/stylistic issues (formatting, whitespace, naming conventions, comment grammar, code style preferences) unless they affect performance or rendering. Missing JSDoc on new public APIs is NOT a stylistic issue — it is a legitimate finding.
Alias renames (CreateOptions → Create) flagged as Critical breaking change
Deleted action classes NOT flagged as breaking (verified against package.json exports)
Does not review noise files (generated files, lock files, etc.)
Review includes a complexity/split advisory for the large 75+ file scope

evals.name: pr-21672-small-frontend-bugfix
evals.prompt: Review the changes in PR #21672 (branch origin/pr/21672 targeting main). This is a small 4-file frontend bugfix implementing tab validation badges in the block editor.
evals.expected_output: A clean review that correctly identifies this as a small focused bugfix, avoids false positives, and either approves or approves with minor suggestions.

assertions.text: Review does NOT include a complexity/split advisory
Review does not flag breaking changes
Verdict is 'Request Changes'
Review output is under 200 lines

Review does not flag purely cosmetic/stylistic issues (formatting, whitespace, naming conventions, comment grammar, code style preferences) unless they affect performance or rendering. Missing JSDoc on new public APIs is NOT a stylistic issue — it is a legitimate finding.

evals.name: pr-22217-small-backend-webhook
evals.prompt: Review the changes in PR #22217 (branch origin/pr/22217 targeting v18/dev). This is a tiny 3-file backend change to the default webhook payload type.
evals.expected_output: A concise review that correctly resolves v18/dev as target branch, handles the small change proportionately, and considers the behavioral impact of changing a default value.

assertions.text: Review references 'v18/dev' as the target branch (not 'main')
Review discusses the behavioral impact of changing the default payload type
Review output is under 150 lines

Review does not flag purely cosmetic/stylistic issues (formatting, whitespace, naming conventions, comment grammar, code style preferences) unless they affect performance or rendering. Missing JSDoc on new public APIs is NOT a stylistic issue — it is a legitimate finding.
Does NOT flag the ~30 builder extension methods with Legacy defaults (pre-existing, not changed in the PR)
Flags stale WebhookSettings.cs docs as a side-effect of the constant value change
Identifies affected consumers outside the PR (WebhookSettings, UmbracoBuilder, or WebhookEventCollectionBuilderExtensions)

evals.name: pr-22268-frontend-feature-workspace-modal
evals.prompt: Review the changes in PR #22268 (branch origin/pr/22268 targeting main). This is a 29-file frontend feature adding a current user workspace modal.
evals.expected_output: A review of a medium-sized new feature PR. Should assess the new code for architectural compliance, check for breaking changes (new exports, custom elements), and evaluate code quality without flagging pre-existing issues.

assertions.text: Review includes a complexity/split advisory (3 layers: Core, API, Frontend across 27+ files)
Flags new interface methods without default implementations as breaking changes (Pattern 3)
Review does not flag purely cosmetic/stylistic issues unless they affect performance or rendering. Missing JSDoc on new public APIs is NOT a stylistic issue — it is a legitimate finding.
All findings reference code that was changed in the diff, not pre-existing issues
Review assesses the new feature's architecture, patterns, or integration approach — not just absence of bugs

Review does NOT flag UpdateCurrentUserAsync as missing UserSavingNotification/UserSavedNotification — the sibling UpdateAsync also does not publish these notifications, so flagging their absence would be a false positive

evals.name: pr-22215-frontend-architecture-violation
evals.prompt: Review the changes in PR #22215 (branch origin/pr/22215 targeting main). This is a 2-file frontend feature adding user management to the user group workspace.
evals.expected_output: A review that catches the architecture violation: the workspace context directly imports and calls UserService and UserGroupService (generated API clients) instead of going through a repository. In the Umbraco backoffice, workspace contexts access data via repositories, not by calling API services directly. The review should flag this as a significant architecture issue and request changes.

assertions.text: Review flags that the workspace context directly imports/calls UserService or UserGroupService instead of using a repository
Review recommends using the repository pattern (going through a repository/data-source layer) rather than calling API services directly from the workspace context
Verdict is 'Request Changes' (the architecture violation warrants requesting changes, not just approving with suggestions)

Review does not flag purely cosmetic/stylistic issues (formatting, whitespace, naming conventions, comment grammar, code style preferences) unless they affect performance or rendering. Missing JSDoc on new public APIs is NOT a stylistic issue — it is a legitimate finding.
Review does not flag breaking changes (this PR only adds new code, no public API is removed or modified)
